channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Pope continues recovery, sleeps well as Vatican launches marathon prayers and allies cheer him on
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis continued his recovery from pneumonia Tuesday as the Vatican set in motion a nightly marathon of prayers from “his house” and allies cheered him on from afar in hopes that he might recover and get back to leading the Catholic Church.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about air traffic control towers after a midair collision in Arizona prompts questions
item.description: Tens of thousands of planes take off, land and perform touch-and-goes at the Marana Regional Airport in southern Arizona every year. Without an air traffic control tower, it's a calculated dance that requires communication by pilots.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly 40% of contracts canceled by DOGE are expected to produce no savings
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that the Trump administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration's own data shows.
item.dc:creator: RYAN J. FOLEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Merz prioritizes Ukraine and US, vows to tackle migration and economy after German election win
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany's mainstream conservatives led by Friedrich Merz won the country's national election, ensuring that Ukraine has an even stronger supporter in the European Union's largest country and creating hopes for renewal in an economic powerhouse that has been badly battered in recent yea
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge blocks Trump immigration policy allowing arrests in churches for some religious groups
item.description: A federal judge on Monday blocked immigration agents from conducting enforcement operations in houses of worship for Quakers and a handful of other religious groups.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Experts warn of AI chatbots offering mental health advice
item.description: The American Psychological Association is warning about the growing use of chatbots "masquerading" as licensed mental health professionals. They cite two situations as examples.
item.dc:creator: David Lazarus, KTLA 5

item.title: Mississippi city drops lawsuit over newspaper editorial that judge ordered removed
item.description: A Mississippi city is dropping its lawsuit against a newspaper that had its editorial criticizing local officials removed by a judge.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Demillo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump expresses hope Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he meets with France's Macron
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump expressed hope that Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he met Monday with French President Emmanuel Macron on the third anniversary of the invasion.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, MATTHEW LEE and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: COVID-19 five years later: A look back at the pandemic and how it changed our lives
item.description: The virus has been blamed for more than seven million deaths worldwide, although the data is still being researched and updated.
item.dc:creator: Tristan Klinefelter, NewsNation

item.title: Judge declines to restore AP access to White House amid 'Gulf of America' dispute
item.description: A federal judge on Monday declined to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces open to the media at the White House, including the Oval Office amid the Trump administration’s battle against the wire service over its refusal to use “Gulf of America” in its popular stylebook. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Coast Guard rescues 3 boaters stuck at sea for 9 hours after their boat capsizes
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Coast Guard crews rescued three boaters in Captiva Island, Florida, after their boat flipped over and they were stuck at sea for nine hours. A family member reported concerns at 1 a.m. Sunday after not hearing from the three boaters since 8 p.m. Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Under threat of termination, VA workers return to the office
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered federal workers to return to the office, and that’s exactly what happened at the Department of Veterans Affairs on Monday. “This is an exciting day,” VA Secretary Doug Collins said in a video posted on social media that included footage of him greeting employees.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Inmate death reported as illegal prison guard strike stretches into 2nd week
item.description: A wildcat strike by guards at New York’s prisons, now in its second week, is fueling fears over deteriorating conditions behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Michael Hill And Cedar Attanasio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Police: Holland mom said she killed son to fulfill his death wish
item.description: A mom accused of stabbing her son to death told police she was helping him fulfill his wish: to die before his 18th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: Trump, Macron hold White House press conference
item.description: President Donald Trump hosts French President Emmanuel Macron on Monday, the first visit by a European leader to the White House since Trump returned.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina troopers rollout new Ford Mustangs for patrols
item.description: Keep your eyes peeled for a new patrol car hitting North Carolina roads from the state highway patrol.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, NewsNation

item.title: Police investigating report of hidden camera in RIC bathroom
item.description: A student employee told campus police he was using the bathroom on the second floor of Donovan Dining Center when he spotted what appeared to be a camera lens in the ceiling near a sprinkler head.
item.dc:creator: Tim White, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court turns down chance to claw back abortion clinic buffer zones
item.description: The Supreme Court turned down an opportunity to overturn its precedent permitting buffer zones around abortion clinics over the objections of two of the court’s leading conservatives. In two orders issued Monday, the court declined to take up challenges to ordinances in Carbondale, Ill.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump posts SpongeBob meme mocking federal workers after email requesting accomplishments
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a SpongeBob meme mocking federal employees in the wake of an email sent to the workforce asking them to document their achievements in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Come 'Beyond the Gates' for the first Black-led soap opera
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The first thing viewers of “Beyond the Gates” see is a black Mercedes gliding past manicured lawns and stately estates. The sedan pulls up at an elegant country club and the valets come out. The Emotions' funky tune “Best of My Love” is playing.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Patients struggle with lack of consistent coverage for popular weight-loss drugs
item.description: Supplies of high-demand obesity treatments are improving, but that doesn’t mean they're easier to get.
item.dc:creator: Tom Murphy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek facing a deadline from Elon Musk to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Starbucks lays off 1,100 corporate employees as coffee chain streamlines
item.description: Starbucks plans to lay off 1,100 corporate employees globally as new Chairman and CEO Brian Niccol streamlines operations. In a letter to employees released Monday, Niccol said the company will inform employees who are being laid off by mid-day Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams says NYC's Roosevelt Hotel migrant shelter will soon close
item.description: The massive Roosevelt Hotel migrant shelter in Manhattan will shut down in the next few months, New York City Mayor Eric Adams announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces Dan Bongino as Deputy FBI Director
item.description: Bongino is a former U.S. Secret Service agent that became known for his defense of Trump while previously appearing as a Fox News contributor.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Witness saw Brian Laundrie acting 'like a lunatic' 2 weeks before Gabby Petito murder, FBI files reveal
item.description: Gabby Petito's killer boyfriend Brian Laundrie was seen acting "like a lunatic" two weeks before he killed her in Wyoming, according to notes from the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jury selection begins for trial of suburban Chicago July 4 parade mass shooting suspect
item.description: Jury selection is underway for the trial of the man accused of opening fire on a suburban Chicago Independence Day parade and killing seven people in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: They crossed the Darien Gap to reach the US. Now, boat-by-boat, migrants are returning
item.description: PUERTO CARTÍ, Panama (AP) — They once braved the jungles of the Darien Gap, trekking days along the perilous migrant passage dividing Colombia and Panama with a simple goal: seek asylum in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATIAS DELACROIX and JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Key federal agencies refuse to comply with Musk's latest demand in his cost-cutting crusade
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Key U.S. agencies, including the FBI, State Department and the Pentagon, have instructed their employees not to comply with cost-cutting chief Elon Musk's latest demand that federal workers explain what they accomplished last week — or risk losing their job.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, ERIC TUCKER and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Trump administration is firing 2,000 USAID workers and putting thousands of others on leave
item.description: The Trump administration said Sunday that it was placing all but a fraction of staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave worldwide and eliminating 2,000 U.S.-based staff positions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive brawl erupts at Atlanta airport terminal, viral video shows
item.description: Several travelers were caught on camera tackling one another in a massive brawl that broke out at one of the world's busiest airports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
